bibliographic_data.title: Multivariate Functional Data Modeling with Time-varying Clustering
bibliographic_data.abstract: We consider the situation where multivariate functional data has been collected over time at each of a set of sites. Our illustrative setting is bivariate, monitoring ozone and PM10 levels as a function of time over the course of a year at a set of monitoring sites. Our objective is to implement model-based clustering of the functions across the sites. Using our example, such clustering can be considered for ozone and PM10 individually or jointly. It may occur differentially for the two pollutants. More importantly for us, we allow that such clustering can vary with time.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper proposes a model-based clustering approach for multivariate functional data across sites, allowing the clustering to vary with time.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors use a multivariate Gaussian process to model the functions and the Dirichlet process to cluster the functions based on their response to exogenous variables. They employ dimension reduction techniques to handle high-dimensional data.
outcomes: Developed a method for clustering multivariate functional data across sites.
Allowed for time-varying clustering of ozone and PM10 levels over monitoring sites.
core_content.full_text_markdown: The paper addresses the problem of clustering multivariate functional data collected over time at multiple sites. Specifically, it focuses on ozone and PM10 levels monitored hourly in 24 sites in Mexico City for the year 2017. The authors propose a model-based approach using a multivariate Gaussian process to handle high-dimensional data and the Dirichlet process for clustering functions based on their response to exogenous variables. Time-varying clustering is achieved by partitioning the time scale, addressing computational challenges associated with continuous-time clustering.
